title: endiandra muelleri
intro: Endiandra muelleri, also known as the Queensland walnut or forest walnut, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lauraceae, native to Australia and New Guinea. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, with its bark, leaves, and seeds used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Endiandra muelleri include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions, as well as its ability to support immune function and protect against infection. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of decoctions, infusions, or tinctures, and is sometimes combined with other medicinal plants to enhance its therapeutic effects.
